results.title: Marcos heads to Japan for four-day state visit
results.description: MANILA, Philippines — President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos left for Tokyo, Japan on Tuesday for a four-day state visit. The plane carrying Marcos, First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos, and the rest of the Philippine delegation departed the Villamor Airbase in Pasay City at 1:36 p.m. Their estimated time of arrival at Haneda Airport is 6:15 p.m. (local time). This marks the President’s first state visit to the East Asian country. READ: Marcos, Sakaichi to raise Japanese arms imports in Tokyo state visit During the visit, the Philippines and Japan are expected to yield new agreements on defense, trade [...]... Keep on reading: Marcos heads to Japan for four-day state visit

results.title: Freedom Day exposes Zambia’s deepening political divide
results.description: President Hakainde Hichilema and opposition leaders used Africa Freedom Day to send sharply different messages to the country, laying bare just how divided Zambia is heading into the August 13 general election. This division highlights the importance of achieving true Freedom in all aspects of society. At State House and the Freedom Statue in Lusaka, [...]

results.title: Funeral By-Laws Ignite Uproar in Mzuzu: Order or Overreach?
results.description: Newly introduced community by-laws in Mzuzu are stirring heated debate across civil society, religious institutions and traditional leadership circles, with funeral-related regulations at the centre of controversy. The Mzuzu City Council, working with local stakeholders, recently launched 18 community by-laws aimed at restoring order in urban neighbourhoods.
